title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your doctor may recommend you get a carotid and dardirectomy if your carotid arteries are narrowed or blocked with plaque.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen rich blood from your heart to your brain. You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Three deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery. These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow to the brain. This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a TIA, may occur. When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: A carotid and dardirectomy is usually done under general anesthesia, which will put you to sleep for the duration of the procedure. In this case, a breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during the operation.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Sometimes a carotid and dardirectomy is done with local anesthesia. If local anesthesia is used, you will remain awake, but your neck will be numbed. You will probably also receive some sedation.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: On the side of your neck along the blocked artery, the surgeon will make an incision that may run from just behind the ear to a point above the collarbone.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will find the carotid artery and temporarily clamp off blood flow through

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will open up the artery and may place a shunt or temporary bypass to keep blood flowing to the brain.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Your surgeon will then remove the plaque within the arterial walls.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: After the blockage has been removed, here she will sew the artery back together, usually placing a patch to widen the artery slightly. The patch may be made of part of one of your veins or an artificial material such as

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: Finally, your surgeon will close the incision with sutures or staples and cover it with A temporary drain is frequently left in the wound.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
text_en: After your operation, you will be taken to a recovery area where the staff will monitor your vital signs and neurologic condition. You will be given pain medication as needed and will gradually progress from a liquid to a solid diet.
